methods.description: Procedure for collecting Scots pine samples and determining annual needle production (PROD) based on measured shoot length and needle density, following standardized protocols.

steps.description: Ten Scots pine trees from each of the five sites along the transect (Rovaniemi (F1), Sodankylä (F2), Laanila (F3), Kaamanen (F4) and Kevo (F5)) from the Arctic Circle to the northern tree line of pine in northernmost Finland (Fig. 2, Table 1) were felled in September 1996 for needle-production analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: Fell Scots pine trees from specified sites for sampling

steps.description: To extend the chronologies five additional pines per site were sampled in Rovaniemi and Laanila in September 2000.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample additional Scots pine trees from specified sites

steps.description: Annual samples were prepared and treated according to NTM standardized protocol (Aalto andJalkanen 1998, Jalkanen et al. 2000).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and treat annual tree samples according to NTM standardized protocol

steps.description: Needle production (see Jalkanen et al. 2002, referred to hereafter as PROD) is determined as the number of short shoots produced annually in a long shoot, and it is calculated based on measured shoot length and needle density (Jalkanen et al. 1998). PROD has been recorded from the main stem (rather than from branches), which has been shown to have many advantages (Jalkanen et al. 2000).
steps.searchable_summary: Determine needle production from the main stem
outputs: Identified main stem sections for analysis

steps.description: Needle production (see Jalkanen et al. 2002, referred to hereafter as PROD) is determined as the number of short shoots produced annually in a long shoot, and it is calculated based on measured shoot length and needle density (Jalkanen et al. 1998).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate needle production (PROD) based on measured shoot length and needle density

methods.description: Procedure for monitoring annual pollen accumulation using modified Tauber traps and calculating the pollen accumulation rate (PAR) for specific pollen types, following standardized procedures.

steps.description: Annual pollen-accumulation was monitored by means of modified Tauber traps following the standard procedure of the Pollen Monitoring Programme (PMP, Hicks et al. 1996Hicks et al. , 1999)).
steps.searchable_summary: Monitor annual pollen accumulation using modified Tauber traps following PMP standard procedure

steps.description: Two transect series of traps were used from the Arctic Circle to the northern timberline. In the first set (Apukka (A5), Petkula (S21), Ukonjärvi (S22), Palomaa (P9) and Kevo (Ke8) (Fig. 2, Table 1), each trap was situated in the centre of a small mire (c.200 m in diameter), which formed an opening in the regional forest. In the second set for the short-term pollen monitoring (code numbers F2, F3, F4 and F5 respectively), each trap was placed within the same forest (no sample for F1) from which the trees for the needle-production analyses were felled (Fig. 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Place pollen traps in specific locations (mire centers, within forests)
inputs: Trap locations (A5, S21, S22, P9, Ke8, F2, F3, F4, F5)

steps.description: The whole range of pollen taxa collected in each trap was identified...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify all pollen taxa collected in each trap
outputs: Identified pollen taxa list per sample

steps.description: ...but only the results for the Pinus diploxylon pollen type are presented here (by default Scots pine because the only other diploxylon Pinus species, the introduced Pinus contorta (Dougl.)Loud., has a very limited distribution, occurring just in the southernmost part of the monitoring transect).
steps.searchable_summary: Filter analysis to focus on Pinus diploxylon pollen type

steps.description: The amount of pollen accumulated was calculated relative to added marker grains (Stockmarr 1971, Maher 1981)...
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate amount of pollen accumulated relative to added marker grains
inputs: Known amount of added marker grains

steps.description: ...and expressed as grains cm -2 year -1 (referred to hereafter as pollen accumulation rate, PAR; for error ranges on these calculations, see Hicks 2001).
steps.searchable_summary: Express pollen accumulation as grains cm^-2 year^-1 (PAR)

methods.method_name: PROD and PAR Chronology Analysis and PAR Reconstruction
methods.description: Statistical analysis of PROD and PAR chronologies and their relationship with temperature, including de-trending, pre-whitening, cross-correlation, and time-series regression to reconstruct past PAR.

steps.description: The longest PROD chronologies date back to 1948.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain PROD chronologies

steps.description: The common period for the two proxies (PROD and PAR) is 19 years long, 1982-2000. ... Measured PAR series passed both white noise and unit-root tests and were used without transformations to produce PAR chronologies.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain PAR chronologies

steps.description: Meteorological data from the nearest climate stations were used as follows: Apukka for F1 and A5, Sodankylä for F2 and S21, Ivalo airport for F3, S22, F4 and P9, and Kevo for F5 and Ke8. Meteorological data of the years 1961-2000 and 1980-2001 were used for PROD and PAR, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain mean monthly temperature data from climate stations for specified periods
inputs: Climate station names (Apukka, Sodankylä, Ivalo airport, Kevo)

steps.description: The relationship between PROD on the one hand and PAR on the other, and the mean monthly temperatures was analysed for the two previous biological years, i.e. from October to September.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze the relationship between PROD/PAR and mean monthly temperatures for previous biological years

steps.description: Prior cross-correlation analysis, PROD series were de-trended... In the two-phase de-trending a linear regression was fit to individual series...
steps.searchable_summary: De-trend individual PROD series using linear regression

steps.description: ...and thereafter a 21-26-year (50% of the series length) cubic spline was applied to remove higher frequency variation in the series.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply cubic spline to de-trended PROD series to remove higher frequency variation

steps.description: ...and also pre-whitened if a white noise test indicated statistically significant autocorrelation.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform white noise test on PROD series to check for autocorrelation
outputs: White noise test result (indication of significant autocorrelation)

steps.description: If necessary, the series were pre-whitened using the autocorrelation-modelling feature of program ARSTAN. This selects the appropriate model based on the minimum Akaike information criterion.
steps.searchable_summary: Pre-whiten PROD series using ARSTAN autocorrelation modelling based on AIC if autocorrelation is significant

steps.description: The mean indexed PROD for each year (PROD chronology) was calculated from the residual values using the bi-weight robust mean method (Cook et al. 1990).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the mean indexed PROD chronology from residual values using the bi-weight robust mean method
inputs: Residual PROD values (after de-trending and pre-whitening)

steps.description: Measured PAR series passed both white noise and unit-root tests and were used without transformations to produce PAR chronologies.
steps.searchable_summary: Use measured PAR series without transformations to produce PAR chronologies

steps.description: PROD and PAR chronologies were crosscorrelated with mean monthly temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: Cross-correlate PROD and PAR chronologies with mean monthly temperatures

steps.description: Time-series regression analysis was applied when reconstructing the past PAR.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply time-series regression analysis to reconstruct past PAR

steps.description: Either PROD chronology or temperature were used as independent variables.
steps.searchable_summary: Select independent variable (PROD chronology or temperature) for PAR reconstruction model

steps.description: Recorded PAR (the dependent variable) was, therefore, centered and log-transformed prior to the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Center the recorded PAR (dependent variable)

steps.description: Recorded PAR (the dependent variable) was, therefore, centered and log-transformed prior to the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Log-transform the centered PAR (dependent variable)

steps.description: The parameters of the models were solved using AUTOREG procedure of SAS statistical software (SAS User's Guide 2001). As a result, the reconstruction model followed a simple exponential form: where Y t is the recorded PAR of year t, Y is the mean PAR of the stand, X t is an independent variable (current-year PROD or the average temperature of the previous July), and a and b are parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate parameters (a, b) of the exponential reconstruction model using SAS AUTOREG procedure

steps.description: Each stand was analysed separately.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform analysis procedures separately for each stand
